Rate the quality of the following neuroimaging study summary on a scale of 1-10.

Author: Dr. Emily Harrington, Cognitive Neuroscience Lab

Using high-resolution 7T fMRI, we identified a novel functional connectivity pattern between the anterior insula and dorsolateral prefrontal cortex during decision-making under uncertainty. The study (n=94) employed a Bayesian hierarchical model to separate individual differences in risk sensitivity from task-specific neural activation. Results show that connectivity strength in this pathway predicts real-world financial risk-taking behavior (r=0.41, p<0.001) beyond what is captured by standard self-report measures. Cross-validation on a held-out sample (n=38) confirmed the predictive model's generalizability.
